entries.summary: Reports of attacks on Hindu homes and temples emerged hours after Hasina fled Dhaka.
Bangladesh Hindu Buddhist Christian Unity Council said hundreds of Hindu properties had been attacked.
Muhammad Yunus took oath as chief adviser of Bangladesh's interim government in Dhaka.
Hindus in Dhaka and other cities staged rallies demanding protection from communal violence.
Yunus said attacks on Hindus were heinous and called for unity across faiths.
Yunus visited Dhakeshwari National Temple and met representatives of the Hindu community.
Bangladesh police and army said most reported violence was political, not purely communal.
Bangladesh police set additional security for the upcoming Durga Puja festival.
Bangladesh marked the start of Durga Puja under heightened security at temple venues.
Bangladesh arrested Hindu monk Chinmoy Krishna Das at Dhaka airport on sedition charges.
Clashes erupted at Chittagong court premises after Chinmoy Krishna Das's bail was denied.
Lawyer Saiful Islam Alif was killed in the Chittagong court-area clashes.
India's foreign ministry said it was deeply concerned by the arrest of the Hindu monk.
Protesters in Agartala breached Bangladesh's assistant high commission compound briefly.
Bangladesh summoned India's envoy to protest the Tripura mission breach as unacceptable.
Bangladesh suspended consular services in Agartala citing the security incident.
Indian Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri visited Dhaka for talks with Yunus's government.
Bangladesh said many viral claims of attacks on Hindus had been fact-checked as misleading.
Hindu homes, businesses and temples were reportedly targeted in Bangladesh after Hasina's fall.
Bangladesh Hindu Buddhist Christian Unity Council reported widespread attacks on minority families.
Muhammad Yunus was sworn in as the interim government's chief adviser.
Hindus rallied across Bangladesh asking the new authorities for security guarantees.
Yunus condemned attacks on Hindus as a heinous offence and urged calm.
Yunus visited a major Dhaka Hindu temple in a public show of solidarity.
Bangladesh authorities said most reported attacks were politically rather than religiously motivated.
Durga Puja festivities began across Bangladesh under heavy security at temples.
Hindu leader Chinmoy Krishna Das was arrested in Dhaka on sedition-related charges.
Clashes broke out at Chittagong court after the monk was denied bail.
A lawyer was killed during the violence outside the Chittagong court.
India said it was deeply concerned about the monk's arrest and minority safety.
Bangladesh said India's statement was an interference in its internal affairs.
Protesters in India's Tripura state breached a Bangladesh diplomatic compound.
India said it regretted the unfortunate Agartala incident at the Bangladesh mission.
Indian Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri held meetings with Yunus and his foreign adviser.
Misri said India had raised attacks on minorities and cultural property with Dhaka.
